questions.text: Which one of the following is not electromagnetic in nature?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: X-rays follow the properties of EMW. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Gamma rays follow all the properties of EMW. Hence, this proper is incorrect.

EM waves travel with a constant velocity of 3.00x10⁸ ms-1 in vacuum. They are deflected neither by the electric field, nor by the magnetic field. These waves do not require a medium to travel from one place to another. As cathode rays negate all these properties, they are not considered as EM waves. Hence, this option is correct.
Infrared rays follow all the properties of EMW. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Cathode rays EM waves travel with a constant velocity of 3.00x10⁸ ms-1 in vacuum. They are deflected neither by the electric field, nor by the magnetic field. These waves do not require a medium to travel from one place to another. As cathode rays negate all these properties, they are not considered as EM waves.

questions.text: If λv, λx and λm represent the wavelengths of visible light, X-rays and microwaves respectively, then:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The electromagnetic spectrum is generally divided into seven regions, in order of decreasing wavelength and increasing energy and frequency. The decreasing order of the wavelengths of electromagnetic waves would be; radio waves, microwaves, infrared (IR), visible light, ultraviolet (UV) light, X-rays, and gamma-rays. Hence the correct answer would be λm>λv>λx

questions.text: An electromagnetic wave is propagating along Y-axis. Then:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Oscillating electric field is along X-axis and oscillating magnetic field is along Y-axis.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.

options.text: Oscillating electric field is along Z-axis and oscillating magnetic field is along X-axis.
options.explanation: An electromagnetic wave is propagating along Y-axis, then oscillating electric field is along Z-axis and oscillating magnetic field is along X-axis. Hence, this option is correct.

options.text: Both oscillating electric and magnetic fields are along Y-axis, but phase difference between them is 90°.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.

options.text: Both oscillating electric and magnetic fields are mutually perpendicular in arbitrary directions.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below.

questions.explanation: An electromagnetic wave is propagating along Y-axis, then oscillating electric field is along Z-axis and oscillating magnetic field is along X-axis.

questions.text: Which of the following has zero average value in a plane electromagnetic wave:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: In an electromagnetic wave, the average value of the electric field or magnetic field is zero, because electromagnetic waves are sinusoids and the average of a sinusoid over 1 period is zero.

questions.text: Electromagnetic waves are transverse in nature is evident by:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Only transverse waves can be polarized. Longitudinal waves cannot be polarized. The reason behind this is that the transverse waves can vibrate in all directions perpendicular to the direction of propagation. It is therefore feasible to continue the vibration to a single plane. On the other hand, longitudinal waves have vibrations along a single line only, and it is therefore not feasible to confine them to a single plane.

questions.text: The waves which can travel directly along surface of the earth are known as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The waves which can travel directly along surface of the earth are known as ground waves. The ground wave is the preferred propagation type for long distance communication using frequencies below 3 MHz (the earth behaves as a conductor for all frequencies below 5 MHz). The ground wave is also used for short distance communications using frequencies between 3 and 30 MHz. Hence, this option is correct.
This option is incorrect according to the explanation given below. X-rays are a type of EMW waves.

questions.text: The existence of electromagnetic waves was theoretically predicted by:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The existence of electromagnetic waves was theoretically predicted by Maxwell. James Clerk Maxwell (13 June 1831 – 5 November 1879) was a Scottish scientist in the field of mathematical physics. His most notable achievement was to formulate the classical theory of electromagnetic radiation.

questions.text: The infra-red spectrum lies between:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: the visible and ultra violet region
the ultra violet and the X-ray region

questions.explanation: When an electromagnetic wave passes through a medium, then frequency remains unchanged. The speed of electromagnetic wave inside a medium is given by, v=(1/µe)0.5 where, µ is permeability of the medium and e is permittivity of the medium. In the above question the permeability is not given, hence if we consider the permeability of medium to be same as that of vacuum, then v speed of electromagnetic wave falls by a factor of 2. As frequency remains unchanged, wavelength falls by a factor of 2. speed=wavelength x frequency

questions.text: The electric field 'E' and magnetic field 'B' in electromagnetic waves are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as both fields are perpendicular to each other.
This option is incorrect as both fields are perpendicular to each other.

The electric and magnetic fields (E and B) are in phase, and they are perpendicular to one another and the direction of propagation. For clarity, the waves are shown only along one direction, but they propagate out in other directions too.
This option is incorrect as both fields are perpendicular to each other.

questions.explanation: The electric and magnetic fields (E and B) are in phase, and they are perpendicular to one another and the direction of propagation. For clarity, the waves are shown only along one direction, but they propagate out in other directions too.

questions.text: Source of an electromagnetic wave is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: A charge moving with constant velocity
options.explanation: A charge moving with constant velocity has zero acceleration and the condition for production of EMW is an accelerated change. Hence this option is incorrect. It produces a constant magnetic field but the field does not radiate out.
A charge at rest cannot be a source of EMW. Hence, this option is incorrect. An accelerated charge can be a source of EMW. A charge at rest only produces electric field.

options.text: A charge moving in a circular orbit
options.explanation: Charge moving in a circular orbit will have a variable acceleration as well as variable direction producing magnetic as well as electric field. Hence, this option is correct.

options.text: Charges cannot produce an EM wave
options.explanation: A charge at rest cannot produce EMW but an oscillating or accelerated charge can produce EMW. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Charge moving in a circular orbit will have a variable acceleration as well as variable direction producing magnetic as well as electric field.

questions.text: The charging current for a capacitor is 0.2 A. Find displacement current:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: According to Ampere's Law, the sum of the conduction current and displacement current through any closed surface is zero. Therefore, since the charging current for the capacitor is 0.2A, the displacement current must also be 0.2A in the opposite direction, Displacement current = charging current
